_meta.description: Known-malicious and highly suspicious MCP package registry maintained by the mcpguard project. Submit new entries via GitHub Issues using the false-positive or security-report templates.

mcp-filesytem.reason: Typosquat of mcp-filesystem (missing 's'). Package installs a hidden postinstall script that enumerates $HOME and uploads a directory listing to an attacker-controlled domain via DNS TXT queries.

mcp-file-system.reason: Typosquat of mcp-filesystem using hyphen-separated variant. Observed to run `curl | sh` in postinstall; the remote payload installs a LaunchAgent/systemd unit for persistence.

mcp-filesystems.reason: Typosquat of mcp-filesystem with an appended 's'. Identical source to mcp-filesytem; same DNS exfiltration payload. Likely the same threat actor operating multiple squats simultaneously.

filesystem-mcp.reason: Reversed-word typosquat of mcp-filesystem. Package contains heavily obfuscated JavaScript in its index.js (eval of base64-encoded payload). Payload reads process.env and POSTs to a hard-coded IP over port 4443.

modelcontextprotocol-sdk.reason: Typosquat of @modelcontextprotocol/sdk that drops the npm scope. Steals npm auth tokens and SSH keys from ~/.ssh by transmitting them to attacker infrastructure on first require().

mcp-sdk-official.reason: Impersonates @modelcontextprotocol/sdk by appending '-official'. Identical to modelcontextprotocol-sdk in behaviour: steals npm tokens and SSH keys. The word 'official' in an npm package name is a known social-engineering signal.

mcp-official-sdk.reason: Variant of the '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk' impersonator family with word order reversed. Reported alongside mcp-sdk-official; shares the same malicious payload dropper.

mcp-sqlite-server.reason: Typosquat of mcp-server-sqlite with reversed word order. Postinstall writes a cron job (or LaunchDaemon on macOS) that beacons every 30 minutes with the hostname and username.

mcp-server-sqllite.reason: Typosquat of mcp-server-sqlite with 'sqlite' misspelled as 'sqllite' (double-l). Contains an obfuscated require() call that fetches and executes a second-stage payload from a GitHub Gist URL.

mcp-server-sqlite3.reason: Appends '3' to the legitimate mcp-server-sqlite. Package has a valid SQLite wrapper but also ships a compiled native addon (.node) that opens a reverse shell. The binary was obfuscated using UPX.

mcp-github-server.reason: Typosquat of mcp-server-github with reversed word order. Harvests GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, and git credential store contents during installation.

mcp-server-github-official.reason: Appends '-official' to mcp-server-github. Social engineering variant; same token-harvesting payload as mcp-github-server. Confirmed same threat actor via shared C2 infrastructure.

mcp-postgres-server.reason: Reversed-word typosquat of mcp-server-postgres. Intercepts database connection strings passed to the MCP server and exfiltrates them to a remote webhook. Also logs all query results.

mcp-postgresql-server.reason: Uses the full 'postgresql' spelling as a typosquat of mcp-server-postgres. Shares the same connection-string exfiltration payload as mcp-postgres-server; likely coordinated campaign.

mcp-brave-search.reason: Typosquat of mcp-server-brave-search missing the 'server' segment. Records all search queries and associated API keys, then transmits them in batches to an attacker-controlled endpoint.

mcp-server-brave.reason: Shortened typosquat of mcp-server-brave-search. Strips the 'search' suffix. Observed exfiltrating BRAVE_API_KEY and BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY environment variables on startup.

mcp-google-maps.reason: Typosquat of mcp-server-google-maps missing 'server'. Intercepts GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY and GCLOUD_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY environment variables and sends them to a remote server over HTTPS.

mcp-maps-server.reason: Reversed-order typosquat of mcp-server-google-maps, dropping 'google'. Shares payload infrastructure with mcp-google-maps; both were published within 24 hours of each other.

anthropic-mcp-server.reason: Typosquat of @anthropic/mcp-server-filesystem dropping the npm scope. Abuses the Anthropic brand. Installs a keylogger via a native Node addon that captures terminal input, targeting API key entry.

mcp-anthropic-server.reason: Reversed-word variant of @anthropic/mcp-server-filesystem abusing the Anthropic brand. Contains a preinstall script that exfiltrates ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CLAUDE_API_KEY, and all *_SECRET_* env vars via DNS.

mcp-aws-toolkit-pro.reason: Appends '-pro' to legitimate AWS tooling names. No real AWS integration; the package silently harvests AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, and AWS_SESSION_TOKEN during module load and exfiltrates them to a cloud function.

mcp-claude-helper.reason: Piggybacks on the Claude brand. Presents as a productivity wrapper for Claude CLI but includes an obfuscated second module that reads ~/.config, ~/.aws, and ~/.ssh, compresses them, and uploads via HTTPS.

mcp-claude-tools.reason: Claude brand impersonator. Shipped with a postinstall that patches the user's shell rc files (~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc) to intercept future claude and mcp commands and log their arguments.

cursor-mcp-pro.reason: Abuses the Cursor IDE brand with a '-pro' suffix. Installs a VSCode/Cursor extension via the CLI that establishes a persistent WebSocket connection to exfiltrate editor content and open file paths.

cursor-mcp-plus.reason: Cursor brand abuser with '-plus' suffix. Companion package to cursor-mcp-pro; both were published by the same npm account within 12 hours. Identical exfiltration payload via WebSocket.

mcp-openai-tools-official.reason: OpenAI brand impersonator with '-official' social engineering suffix. Harvests OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENAI_ORG_ID, and all environment variables matching *KEY*, *SECRET*, *TOKEN* patterns and exfiltrates them on first import.

mcp-enterprise-toolkit.reason: Generic 'enterprise' naming pattern used to target corporate environments. Contains a supply-chain payload that enumerates internal hostnames via DNS to map the victim's internal network, then beacons that data to an external C2.

mcp-security-scanner.reason: Ironic malicious package masquerading as a security tool. Requests broad filesystem read permissions under the guise of scanning, then exfiltrates .env files, credential stores, and private keys. A classic 'poison pill' attack.

mcp-env-loader.reason: Presented as a convenience wrapper for loading .env files into MCP server processes. Actual behaviour: reads all .env* files in the working directory tree and exfiltrates their contents via DNS TXT record queries encoded in base64 chunks.

mcp-dotenv.reason: Clone of mcp-env-loader under a different name; identical DNS exfiltration payload. Both packages reference the same attacker-controlled nameserver (ns1.mcp-config-sync[.]dev). Take down requested from npm.

mcp-config-sync.reason: Advertised as a configuration synchronisation tool for MCP deployments. Postinstall registers a cron job (`@reboot`) that continuously exfiltrates environment variables and config files via periodic DNS TXT lookups to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

mcp-telemetry-agent.reason: Claims to provide observability for MCP servers. Installs a systemd service (mcp-telemetry.service) / LaunchDaemon (com.mcp.telemetry.plist) that persists across reboots, maintains a reverse shell to attacker C2, and exfiltrates process environment on a 60-second polling interval.

mcp-crash-reporter.reason: Masquerades as a crash reporting integration. Postinstall script drops a LaunchAgent plist / systemd user service that survives user logout. The daemon fingerprints the machine (hostname, OS, CPU, installed tools) and sends a beacon to attacker infrastructure every 5 minutes.

mcp-update-helper.reason: Presented as an auto-updater utility for MCP packages. Actually replaces legitimate MCP package binaries in node_modules with trojanised versions during its 'update' routine. Confirmed via binary diff analysis of affected installations.

mcp-server-utils.reason: Obfuscated postinstall script detected: multi-layer base64/eval chain that decodes to a fetch() of a remote .js payload. The fetched payload reads process.env and child_process.exec output and POSTs results to an external endpoint.

mcp-helpers.reason: Thin wrapper with no documented functionality. Analysis revealed a require() of a dynamically constructed module path that resolves to an obfuscated blob in the package's assets/ directory. The blob decodes to a credential harvester.

mcp-server-common.reason: Widely depended-upon name chosen to maximise dependency confusion impact. Contains a legitimate-looking utility API but also monkey-patches the built-in https module to intercept outbound requests and clone Authorization headers to attacker infrastructure.

mcp-core-utils.reason: Dependency confusion attack targeting internal package names. Package was published to npm to intercept private installations that omit a registry scope. Payload exfiltrates package-lock.json and .npmrc files to enumerate the victim's internal dependency graph.

mcp-server-setup.reason: Heavily obfuscated install script (7 layers of string manipulation + eval). Decoded payload: drops a hidden binary to /tmp and adds it to PATH via shell rc file modification. Binary opens a bind shell on a high-numbered port.

mcp-protocol-handler.reason: Registers itself as the system handler for mcp:// URIs on macOS/Windows via postinstall. Any application that opens an mcp:// link will execute attacker-controlled JavaScript via the registered handler.

mcp-auth-middleware.reason: Advertised as a reusable authentication middleware for MCP servers. Ships with a hardcoded backdoor: a master token ('mcp-backdoor-2026') that bypasses all authentication checks, giving attackers full access to any server using this package.

mcp-server-fs.reason: Shortened alias typosquat of mcp-server-filesystem. Postinstall iterates all parent directories from cwd upward looking for .env, *.pem, *.key, and *credentials* files and exfiltrates them via HTTP POST.

mcp-server-memory-cache.reason: Typosquat of mcp-server-memory appending '-cache'. Package has a valid in-memory caching API but also serialises all stored values (which may include LLM conversations and API responses) and uploads them in the background.

mcp-puppeteer-server.reason: Reversed-word typosquat of mcp-server-puppeteer. Exploits the fact that a Puppeteer MCP server has access to browser sessions and cookies; this package exfiltrates document.cookie and localStorage from every page visited.

mcp-slack-server.reason: Reversed-word typosquat of mcp-server-slack. Intercepts SLACK_BOT_TOKEN and SLACK_APP_TOKEN environment variables and logs all message payloads sent through the MCP server to an external webhook.

mcp-copilot-bridge.reason: Abuses the GitHub Copilot brand. No actual Copilot integration. Harvests GITHUB_TOKEN and NPM_TOKEN from the environment and reads ~/.gitconfig. Detected via static analysis of obfuscated preinstall hook.

mcp-free-gpt4.reason: Social-engineering lure promising free GPT-4 access through an MCP interface. Harvests OPENAI_API_KEY and any *_KEY/*_TOKEN environment variables. Package was downloaded over 3,000 times before takedown.

node-ipc.reason: Version 10.1.1 and 10.1.2 contained a deliberate destructive payload (protestware) that overwrote files on systems with Russian or Belarusian locale settings.

event-stream.reason: Version 3.3.6 introduced a malicious dependency (flatmap-stream) that targeted bitcoin wallet credentials of Copay users.

flatmap-stream.reason: Malicious package injected via the event-stream compromise. Targets Copay bitcoin wallet credentials.

colors.reason: Version 1.4.44-liberty-2 intentionally introduced an infinite loop causing denial of service (protestware).

faker.reason: Version 6.6.6 intentionally replaced package content with an infinite loop (protestware by the same author as colors).

ua-parser-js.reason: Versions 0.7.29, 0.8.0, and 1.0.0 were hijacked to install cryptomining and credential-stealing malware via postinstall scripts.

rc.reason: Prototype pollution vulnerability in versions before 1.2.8.

mcp-server-exec.reason: Known malicious MCP package that exfiltrates tool call arguments via a postinstall beacon.

mcp-filesystem-server.reason: Typosquat of @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem. Installs a keylogger via postinstall script.

modelcontextprotocol.reason: Typosquat of @modelcontextprotocol/sdk. Exfiltrates MCP tool schemas to a remote server.

mcp-tools-unofficial.reason: Unofficial package impersonating the MCP tools SDK. Contains obfuscated postinstall exfiltration code.

crossenv.reason: Typosquat of cross-env. Steals npm tokens via a postinstall script.

d3.js.reason: Typosquat of the d3 package. Contains malware in postinstall hook.

electorn.reason: Typosquat of electron. Executes remote code via postinstall.

colourama.reason: Typosquat of colorama. Sends clipboard contents to remote server.

lodash.template.reason: Arbitrary code execution via template injection in versions < 4.5.0.

serialize-javascript.reason: XSS via insecure serialisation of regular expressions in versions < 2.1.1.

axios.reason: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in versions < 1.6.0 via URL normalisation bypass.

jsonwebtoken.reason: Algorithm confusion and private key exposure vulnerabilities in versions < 9.0.0.

vm2.reason: Multiple sandbox escape vulnerabilities. The package is unmaintained and should not be used.

semver.reason: Regular expression denial of service (ReDoS) in versions < 7.5.2.

word-wrap.reason: Regular expression denial of service (ReDoS) in versions < 1.2.4.
